Name: gpu_optimized_inference
Title: Optimizing GPU Memory Bandwidth Utilization for Deep Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging a combination of hardware-aware optimizations, such as optimizing memory access patterns, reducing memory allocation overhead, and exploiting parallelism, we can significantly improve the performance of deep learning inference on GPUs.
Related Work: Previous works have explored optimizing deep learning inference on GPUs, including optimizing memory access patterns, reducing memory allocation overhead, and exploiting parallelism. However, these works have focused on specific aspects of optimization, and a comprehensive approach that combines these techniques is still lacking. Our proposal builds upon the ideas presented in the surveyed papers, particularly the works on optimizing attention mechanisms, leveraging high internal bandwidth in PIM, and optimizing reduction kernels.
Abstract: Deep learning inference on GPUs is a critical component of many applications, including natural language processing, computer vision, and recommender systems. However, the performance of deep learning inference is often limited by the memory bandwidth utilization of the GPU. In this proposal, we aim to optimize the memory bandwidth utilization of GPUs for deep learning inference by leveraging a combination of hardware-aware optimizations. Our approach includes optimizing memory access patterns, reducing memory allocation overhead, and exploiting parallelism. We will evaluate our approach using a range of deep learning models and benchmarks, and we expect to achieve significant improvements in performance and efficiency.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments using a range of deep learning models, including convolutional neural networks, recurrent neural networks, and transformers. We will evaluate the performance of our approach using metrics such as throughput, latency, and memory bandwidth utilization. We will also compare our approach to state-of-the-art baselines and existing optimization techniques. Specifically, we will use the following experiments: (1) optimizing memory access patterns using data layout transformations and memory allocation optimizations, (2) reducing memory allocation overhead using techniques such as memory pooling and allocation caching, and (3) exploiting parallelism using techniques such as parallelizing reduction operations and leveraging multi-threading.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The main risk factors and limitations of our proposal include the complexity of optimizing memory bandwidth utilization, the need for careful tuning of optimization parameters, and the potential for increased power consumption. Additionally, our approach may require significant modifications to the deep learning framework and may not be compatible with all GPU architectures.
